title: To rename the medical center of the Department of Veterans Affairs in Dallas, Texas, as the "Eddie Bernice Johnson VA Medical Center".
plain_summary: This bill changes the name of a veterans' hospital in Dallas, Texas. The hospital will now be called the Eddie Bernice Johnson VA Medical Center. It is being renamed to honor Eddie Bernice Johnson, who worked as a nurse at this hospital for sixteen years and then served veterans as a member of Congress for thirty years. The hospital is located on South Lancaster Road in Dallas.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Health.

sections.title: Section 1  Findings
sections.text: Congress finds these facts about Congresswoman Eddie Bernice Johnson. She worked for sixteen years as the Chief Psychiatric Nurse at the Department of Veterans Affairs medical center in Dallas, Texas. During this time, she served the veteran community with dedication. During her thirty years in Congress, Congresswoman Johnson introduced many bills that were meant to honor and serve the patriots who served their country with distinction. She introduced and got passed the Dr. James Allen Veteran Vision Equity Act of two thousand seven. This law helps those who were wounded while serving in the Armed Forces get the treatment they need. It also increases the respect shown to those who gave their lives in service to their country. Congresswoman Eddie Bernice Johnson was a trailblazer who worked tirelessly for veterans in the United States. She has earned the respect and honor of her home city of Dallas, Texas, the State of Texas, the United States, and Congress.

sections.title: Section 2  Designation of Eddie Bernice Johnson VA Medical Center
sections.text: The medical center of the Department of Veterans Affairs located at four thousand five hundred South Lancaster Road in Dallas, Texas will be renamed. After this law takes effect, it will be known and designated as the Eddie Bernice Johnson Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Center or the Eddie Bernice Johnson VA Medical Center. Any mention in any law, regulation, map, document, paper, or other record of the United States that refers to this medical center will now be considered a reference to the Eddie Bernice Johnson VA Medical Center.
